speaker: interviewer
text: Bernard Presser, tape number seven. Bernard, you've written a statement. I'm wondering if you'd like to read it at this time?

speaker: interviewee
text: OK, well, I'll try my best. 'Oskar Schindler and DEF, how do you have produced anything useful to German. As he inform us, he need times he used this as a cover for him to keep DEF going and getting allotment of shipment iron from-- for producing enamel. Thomas Kennedy did very well bringing Oskar Schindler to general public attention for his human approach to Jew. In such an unbelievable time and for Jew, where only few Gentiles have this courage and determination.

speaker: interviewer
text: What lessons has Mr. Schindler taught you that you still carry with you today? [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, it's a very tricky question. For me, he was example, [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] which I want to follow.

speaker: interviewer
text: I think we've seen from your children and your grandchildren that everybody thinks you have, so--

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: I'd like to thank you at this moment for sharing your story with us. I know it hasn't been easy. Is there anything else you wanted to add?

speaker: interviewee
text: Nothing else.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, we'll stop the tape here, and then we'll show the photographs.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. What's the occasion of this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: 50 anniversary of-- of wedding of my father's parents.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where are your grandparents in the picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: On the bottom-- over the bottom, the lady in the middle.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what is her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Rivka. And is on the right side of her-- or left-- right side of her is her-- her husband, Yunkle.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the last name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yunkle Kempler, [INAUDIBLE] Presser.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. So the gentleman with the beard is your grandfather?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I couldn't tell you that. My father keeped it well because he was not married by this time. So that-- that have to be between hundred 1910 and before 1912. Because my father was married by one-hundred '12.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, and how-- how do you have this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, this family or-- another member of the family-- my father was one of the youngest in the family of 12. Most of them was living in United State. Only in Poland was living four of them, two-- two brothers and two sisters.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where is your father in the photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: On the top, the tallest one with a-- a mustache.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. We'll stop here. And we'll zoom in and get a picture of your father, OK?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: And which one of these is your father?

speaker: interviewee
text: The tallest next to the-- on the-- on the top. On the-- the tallest in-- in this two-- in between two of the people.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is that his brother beside him?

speaker: interviewee
text: Pardon?

speaker: interviewer
text: Is that his brother beside him?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, that's his brother-in-law.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. And your father's name, just to have it with his photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: Chaim.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK, thank you. Who is in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: My mother, her sister, my grandmother, and his-- her second husband.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, maybe you can start from the left and tell me everybody's name, or as many as you can remember.

speaker: interviewee
text: This, my mother's sister [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewee
text: Rose. This, my mother's-- the second standing is [? Mara. ?] In the middle stands my mother.

speaker: interviewer
text: Her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Her name, Frania.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewee
text: There's my grandfather, or secondhand husband of my grandmother.

speaker: interviewer
text: His name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Shia. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewee
text: And my mother sister, Lola. Sitting on the right side is [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] [? Edic ?] Victor, [? Elena. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: The girl in the middle?

speaker: interviewee
text: Grandmother.

speaker: interviewer
text: The grandmother's name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Salika. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: And what is the family name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Troutman. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Have to be taken, I believe, in the '20s before-- because grandfather passed away in '21, between-- after the war. But I couldn't tell you anything because the boys had been very small. And I believe that they have-- I remember had them quite-- quite big there. They are much more quite big than when they were with me.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did anybody from this photo survive the war?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, Lola, my mother, and Victor.

speaker: interviewer
text: That's the one sitting in the front?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. All right, thank you. And who is in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: Sitting is my mother and-- and my-- my-- my husband-- my father is standing behind.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell me their full names, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Frania and Chaim.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In my opinion, it was taken after-- after they had been have a wedding, and between 1912 and '13 somehow.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was it taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Cracow.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Who is in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: Myself when I was 13 in high school, about 13 years old.

speaker: interviewer
text: And let's see, that would be 1927?

speaker: interviewee
text: Something of this kind.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you remember this picture being taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know for what, any occasion, but I believe that we have to have some kind of identification for the school that I-- I was a schoolboy. They have taken, but it's not the right size.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how do you have this picture today?

speaker: interviewee
text: Anything was-- all the pictures what I have have been in place with my friend, [INAUDIBLE] friend. He brought it after the war back.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] he got the back of that one. My daughter-in-law put it in the-- in the right thing. How was she seeing, that was.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thanks. Can you tell me the names of these people?

speaker: interviewee
text: This, our family. On left side is Bernard. Next to is my mother, my sister Bronislawa, my-- my brother Yanek, [? Jonah, ?] and my father.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what year was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: As I can remember, because we-- we both had been-- I mean, [INAUDIBLE], Bronis, junior to me 9 years. So I was about 18, maybe. I don't know exactly because--

speaker: interviewer
text: 18 Would be 19--

speaker: interviewee
text: We didn't take care to remember, to make a note when it  was taken.

speaker: interviewer
text: 18 Would be 1931. Is that about right?

speaker: interviewee
text: '31, '31-- it would be right. It's about that long.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was the picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Cracow.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you. Who is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: Bernard, myself.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yourself. When was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I couldn't tell you. Read it.

speaker: interviewer
text: The date on the back said 1933.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. 1933. This is what is. That was in my cousin's place. They have a they have representative of beer, Gambrinus.

speaker: interviewer
text: That's a beer company?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, there was a small company, beer company, but they make quite good with it.

speaker: interviewer
text: Who took the picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: A cousin of mine.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, OK, thank you. And who is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: Our wedding picture. Rose and myself.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what year was--

speaker: interviewer
text: And where-- where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In some professional one. I couldn't remember where.

speaker: interviewer
text: In which town?

speaker: interviewee
text: Pardon? In Cracow.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, thank you. OK, and who is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: John is on the right side of my-- myself. I am in the middle, and Michael is on the left side sitting. In seaside, I believe that was [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was that?

speaker: interviewee
text: On Baltic Sea.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. And what year was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Pardon?

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was the photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: But looks like John was about six. So baby, Michael was two.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, 1955 about? OK. Alright.

speaker: interviewee
text: I'm not working well.

speaker: interviewer
text: That's-- And what is the occasion of this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Mr. Kennedy book was edited. And on this occasion he come with [INAUDIBLE] to Cracow. And we have gathering of the people-- people who survived with Schindler.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In [INAUDIBLE] Rosner's house.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was that? In which country?

speaker: interviewee
text: Melbourne, Australia.

speaker: interviewer
text: Australia, yeah?

speaker: interviewee
text: Australia.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell me the names of people, maybe start sitting on the bottom, from left to right?

speaker: interviewee
text: On the bottom--

speaker: interviewer
text: Yep.

speaker: interviewee
text: --Hertzog, Mrs. [? Panere, ?] [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Elizar, sister of Mr. [? Panere ?], Helena, Mr. Kennedy, the wife of Pauly Pfefferberg, my wife, Mrs. Elsner, Mrs. Rosner, and Mr. Rosner.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, and now the top row, from left to right?

speaker: interviewee
text: Mr. [INAUDIBLE] [? Panere ?], [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Mr. [INAUDIBLE], who was not in our camp, Mr. Elsner, Bernard Presser.

speaker: interviewer
text: Behind you there is a woman with glasses, can you see her.

speaker: interviewee
text: I-- I-- I couldn't. She was not in with us.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, beside you?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Yashuk, ?] I couldn't remember his name.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the next one?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yusak? How was he named? And his wife, she passed away, Pauly Pfefferberg, Yanek [? Panere ?], person who survived the-- the camp in Mauthausen. Mr. [INAUDIBLE], who is in Melbourne. [INAUDIBLE] I don't remember.

speaker: interviewer
text: So, the one on the end is named?

speaker: interviewee
text: Mr. [? Zisking. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: Mrs. [? Zisking. ?] OK, very good. When was the picture taken? What year?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, I couldn't tell you when because-- that was taken picture--

speaker: interviewer
text: The year the book was launched, yes?

speaker: interviewee
text: Pardon?

speaker: interviewer
text: The year that the book was launched?

speaker: interviewee
text: Some time of this time.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, very good. Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: This [INAUDIBLE] Gross-- Gross. [INAUDIBLE] Gross. I remember the first name. I couldn't remember the surname.

speaker: interviewer
text: In the middle, standing behind the woman?

speaker: interviewee
text: Behind that woman.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, great. You did very well. Thank--
